User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Zuckky AI application, storing their profile and credit balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication. (Relationship: User 1:N Conversation, User 1:N MediaFile, User 1:N CreditTransaction)

name.description: The display name of the user.

currentCreditBalance.description: The current number of editing credits available to the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a single chat session or project where a user interacts with Zuckky AI for video editing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this conversation. (Relationship: User 1:N Conversation)

title.description: A user-defined or system-generated title for the conversation, e.g., 'My first project'.

currentStep.description: The current step in the conversation flow (e.g., 'WELCOME', 'UPLOAD_FOOTAGE', 'SELECT_TEMPLATE', 'AWAITING_CONFIRMATION', 'EDITING_PROCESSING', 'COMPLETED').

activeVideoEditingJobId.description: Optional: Reference to the currently active video editing job within this conversation, if any. (Relationship: Conversation 1:1 VideoEditingJob)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the conversation was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the conversation was last updated.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a conversation, either from the user or the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderType.description: Indicates whether the message was sent by the 'user' or the 'ai'.

contentType.description: The type of content the message represents (e.g., 'text', 'file_reference', 'template_selection', 'summary_request', 'processing_progress', 'edited_video_reference').

text.description: The textual content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent or received.

mediaFileId.description: Optional: Reference to a MediaFile if the message includes an uploaded file. (Relationship: Message 1:1 MediaFile)

editedVideoId.description: Optional: Reference to an EditedVideo if the message includes a preview of a finished video. (Relationship: Message 1:1 EditedVideo)

templateId.description: Optional: Reference to a Template if the message indicates a template selection. (Relationship: Message 1:1 Template)

MediaFile.title: MediaFile
MediaFile.description: Stores metadata about a video file uploaded by a user for editing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaFile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this file. (Relationship: User 1:N MediaFile)

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation in which this file was uploaded. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N MediaFile)

originalFileName.description: The original name of the file as uploaded by the user.

storageUrl.description: The URL or path where the original video file is stored (e.g., S3 URL).

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., 'video/mp4').

sizeBytes.description: The size of the uploaded file in bytes.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the video file in seconds.

uploadTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was uploaded.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a small preview thumbnail image generated from the video.

status.description: The current status of the media file (e.g., 'uploaded', 'processing_thumbnail', 'ready', 'failed').

VideoEditingJob.title: VideoEditingJob
VideoEditingJob.description: Represents a single request for video editing, linking an original media file to specific instructions and resulting in an edited video.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VideoEditingJob entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation that initiated this editing job. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N VideoEditingJob)

originalMediaFileId.description: Reference to the MediaFile (original footage) being edited in this job. (Relationship: VideoEditingJob 1:1 MediaFile)

templateId.description: Optional: Reference to the Template chosen for this editing job. (Relationship: VideoEditingJob 1:1 Template)

customInstructions.description: A comprehensive string containing all AI editing instructions gathered from the user for this job.

customTrainingData.description: Optional: Specific data or reference used for custom training in this job, if applicable.

status.description: The current status of the video editing job (e.g., 'pending', 'analyzing', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled').

progressDetails.description: An array of objects detailing the sequential steps of the editing process with their status.

startedAt.description: Optional: Timestamp indicating when the video editing process officially started.

completedAt.description: Optional: Timestamp indicating when the video editing process was completed.

editedVideoId.description: Optional: Reference to the EditedVideo entity created by this job, once completed. (Relationship: VideoEditingJob 1:1 EditedVideo)

creditCost.description: The number of credits consumed by this video editing job.

EditedVideo.title: EditedVideo
EditedVideo.description: Represents a video that has been processed and edited by Zuckky AI, ready for preview and download.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EditedVideo entity.

videoEditingJobId.description: Reference to the VideoEditingJob that produced this edited video. (Relationship: EditedVideo 1:1 VideoEditingJob)

storageUrl.description: The URL or path where the final edited video file is stored.

previewThumbnailUrl.description: URL to a preview image or a short clip of the edited video.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the edited video in seconds.

fileSizeBytes.description: The size of the edited video file in bytes.

qualityOptions.description: An array of available download quality options for this video (e.g., '1080p', '720p').

downloadCount.description: The number of times this edited video has been downloaded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the edited video was created.

Template.title: Template
Template.description: Defines a predefined video editing style or set of configurations that users can select.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Template entity.

name.description: The name of the template (e.g., 'Hormozi', 'Gadzhi', 'Gary Vee', 'Custom').

description.description: A brief description of the editing style and characteristics of the template.

configurationJson.description: A JSON string containing the specific parameters and configurations for this template, compatible with the editing API (e.g., Submagic).

isActive.description: Indicates whether this template is currently available for users to select.

thumbnailUrl.description: Optional: URL to an image or video showcasing the visual style of the template.

CreditTransaction.title: CreditTransaction
CreditTransaction.description: Records individual credit changes (grants or deductions) for a user's account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CreditTransaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User involved in this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N CreditTransaction)

amount.description: The number of credits added (positive) or deducted (negative) in this transaction.

referenceId.description: Optional: Reference ID to another entity relevant to this transaction (e.g., VideoEditingJob ID for 'video_edit_cost').

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the credit transaction occurred.

description.description: A human-readable description of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Ownership is path-based; the document's ID (`userId`) must match the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`).
Stores individual chat sessions or projects for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path and `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a specific conversation. For authorization independence, documents must include denormalized 'userId' and 'conversationId' fields to match the path and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Stores metadata about video files uploaded within a specific conversation. For authorization independence, documents must include denormalized 'userId' and 'conversationId' fields to match the path and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: mediaFileId
params.description: The unique ID of the uploaded media file.

definition.description: Stores details for individual video editing requests initiated within a conversation. For authorization independence, documents must include denormalized 'userId' and 'conversationId' fields to match the path and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: jobId
params.description: The unique ID of the video editing job.

definition.description: Stores metadata for AI-edited videos resulting from a specific video editing job. For authorization independence, documents must include denormalized 'userId' and 'conversationId' fields to match the path and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: editedVideoId
params.description: The unique ID of the edited video.

definition.description: Records individual credit changes (grants or deductions) for a user's account. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path and `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user associated with the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the credit transaction.

definition.description: Stores global, predefined video editing templates available to all authenticated users. These are read-only for users, with write access restricted to administrators (not defined in this scope).

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique ID of the video editing template.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Zuckky AI is designed to prioritize Authorization Independence, Debuggability, and Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) in alignment with the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** (Mandate A) for all user-owned data, and **Structural Segregation** (Mandate B) for global data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
For all user-specific data, the `userId` (`request.auth.uid`) is directly embedded into the document, even for deeply nested subcollections like `messages`, `videoEditingJobs`, and `editedVideos`. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents within security rules. For example, a `Message` document will not only reside under `/users/{userId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}` but will also contain a `userId` field (and `conversationId` field) within its own data. This allows rules to simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `resource.data.conversationId == conversationId` (from the path), ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** User-specific data is organized hierarchically under `/users/{userId}/...`. This automatically scopes `list` operations. When a user queries `/users/{theirUid}/conversations`, Firestore's implicit filtering (due to path structure) ensures they only see their own conversations. Similarly, for `/users/{theirUid}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`, they can only retrieve messages belonging to their conversation within their user context.
2.  **Structural Segregation:** Global entities like `Templates` are placed in a top-level collection (`/templates`). This allows for simple, broad read rules (e.g., `allow read: if request.auth != null;`) without complex filtering logic, as all documents in this collection share the same security posture.

**Design Justification:**
*   **User-Owned Data:** The hierarchy `/users/{userId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}` provides clear ownership. By denormalizing `userId` and `conversationId` into `Message`, `MediaFile`, `VideoEditingJob`, and `EditedVideo` documents, we achieve complete authorization independence. This means a rule for a `Message` can verify ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`) without having to traverse up to the `Conversation` or `User` document.
*   **Global Data:** `Templates` are system-wide. A flat collection `/templates` provides efficient global access for users to browse available templates, with simple read rules.
*   **Credit Transactions:** `/users/{userId}/creditTransactions/{transactionId}` cleanly segregates financial history for each user, with direct `userId` ownership for easy rule implementation.
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Entities like `Conversation` (with `currentStep`) and `MediaFile`/`VideoEditingJob` (with `status`) use dedicated fields to represent their state, simplifying business logic and rule conditions.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{userId}`, `{conversationId}`). Authorization fields are standardized (e.g., `userId`).
